questions.q: A sports person requires about _______ of water per day.
What is the primary function of carbohydrates in a sports person's diet?
questions.answer: To provide energy for immediate use and also store energy in the form of glycogen.

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of a high-protein food?
Explain the importance of iron in a sports person's diet. (Answer in 50 words)
questions.answer: Iron is essential for transporting oxygen to the muscles. It helps in maintaining healthy red blood cells. Iron deficiency can lead to fatigue, weakness, and decreased performance.

questions.q: A sports person needs _______ of protein per kilogram of body weight.
What is the role of fats in a sports person's diet?
questions.answer: Fats provide energy, help in absorption of vitamins, and maintain healthy skin and hair.

questions.q: Which of the following is a good source of complex carbohydrates?
A sports person should consume _______ of carbohydrates 1-3 hours before exercise.
Explain the concept of 'glycogen loading'. (Answer in 50 words)
questions.answer: Glycogen loading is a technique used to increase muscle glycogen stores. It involves consuming high amounts of carbohydrates 1-3 days before competition or intense exercise.

questions.q: True or False: A sports person should avoid consuming protein before exercise.
